meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kits: Master Debugging with Claude Code
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn how to use SaaS starter kits, Antigravity, and Claude Code to accelerate your development and build professional-grade apps.
content.answer_block: Debugging paralysis stems from manual error tracing. By integrating a robust SaaS starter kit with Claude Code and Antigravity, you transform your workflow. These tools automate the identification of logic flaws, enforce modular architecture, and provide real-time architectural oversight, allowing you to focus on shipping features rather than chasing stack traces.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the cursor blinks, and your codebase feels like a labyrinth with no exit. You’ve spent hours chasing a silent bug, and the frustration is mounting—this is the rite of passage every engineer faces. But while amateurs drown in console logs, professionals build systems that make debugging trivial.

Today, we pivot from manual frustration to automated precision. By leveraging a high-performance SaaS starter kit, you aren't just writing code; you are building on a battle-tested foundation. Combined with the analytical power of Claude Code and Antigravity, you will turn your debugging process into a systematic, high-speed engineering operation.

steps.title: Initialize the Foundation
steps.description: Clone a production-ready SaaS starter kit to ensure your directory structure follows industry standards, which inherently reduces 'spaghetti code' bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this starter kit structure and identify potential bottlenecks in the authentication flow that could lead to runtime errors.

steps.title: Automated Architectural Scan
steps.description: Use Antigravity to map your dependencies and identify circular references before they break your build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Run a dependency audit on the current directory. Highlight any circular imports or deprecated library usage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'review current project structure for circular dependencies'

steps.title: Contextual Bug Isolation
steps.description: Feed specific error logs into Claude Code to get an immediate root-cause analysis rather than guessing line numbers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Here is the stack trace: [INSERT LOG]. Explain the root cause and propose a fix that maintains the existing design pattern.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'fix the bug in auth.ts based on this stack trace: [PASTE LOG]'

steps.title: Unit Test Generation
steps.description: Generate comprehensive test coverage for your new features to prevent regression bugs before they enter production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Jest unit tests for the user-service module, specifically covering edge cases for empty inputs.

steps.title: Refactoring for Maintainability
steps.description: Use AI to refactor complex functions into smaller, testable components, dramatically reducing future debugging time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this complex function into three modular helper functions to improve testability and readability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'refactor the main controller logic for better modularity'

pro_tips: Always define a 'system prompt' for your AI agent that specifies your preferred coding style to ensure consistent refactoring.
Use 'Git bisect' in tandem with Claude Code to isolate the exact commit where the bug was introduced.
Never accept the first AI-generated fix; ask the agent to 'provide three alternative approaches and explain the trade-offs of each'.
Treat your logs as first-class citizens; configure structured logging early in your starter kit to make debugging 10x faster.
Use Antigravity to document your API endpoints automatically as you build; outdated documentation is a primary source of integration bugs.
content.next_steps: Once your project is stable, deploy it to a staging environment and integrate automated CI/CD pipelines to ensure your code remains bug-free as you scale.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the toolkit to outpace the average developer. Stop debugging blindly and start building systems that scale. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
